Getting LLMs to simulate “true” randomness or generate diverse outputs is surprisingly difficult. We found a simple prompting trick that solves this by having the model generate and manipulate a random string. To be presented at #ICLR2026 this week!

A useful ward against slop story/science posts on X is noting which is in the character limit.

All of the models struggle to do 280 character summaries on their first pass, and most of the people creating slop posts that start with 🚨 emojis don’t bother to prompt them better.
